revision.text:
'''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町北仰（きとげ）にある神社である。例祭はサンヤレ祭。旧社格は郷社。
==ご祭神== *主祭神 ** [[角凝魂神]] ** [[紀角宿禰神]] *配祀神 **武内大臣
==ご神紋== 左三ッ巴
==歴史== 創祀年代は不詳であるが、角臣来子宿祢によって勧請されたと伝わる。津野郷および川上郷の惣社。[[明治]]14年に郷社に列格された。[[式内社]]論社である。
==祭事== * サンヤレ祭
==境内社== * 田部神社 * 稲荷社 * 厳島社 * 北野社
==ご神徳== * 五穀豊穣 * 家内安全
== 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:高島市]] [[Category:式内社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=津野神社| 所在地=滋賀県高島市今津町北仰316-1| 祭神=角凝魂神
紀角宿禰神| 社格=式内社
郷社| 創建=創祀年代不詳| 例祭=[[4月18日]]| 神事=}} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町北仰（きとげ）にある神社である。例祭はサンヤレ祭。旧社格は郷社。 ==ご祭神== *主祭神 ** [[角凝魂神]] ** [[紀角宿禰神]] *配祀神 **武内大臣 ==ご神紋== 左三ッ巴 ==歴史== 創祀年代は不詳であるが、角臣来子宿禰によって勧請されたと伝わる。津野郷および河上郷の惣社。[[明治]]14年に郷社に列格された。[[式内社]]論社である。 ==祭事== * サンヤレ祭 ==境内社== * 田部神社 * 稲荷社 * 厳島社 * 北野社 ==ご神徳== * 五穀豊穣 * 家内安全 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:高島市]] [[Category:式内社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=津野神社| 所在地=滋賀県高島市今津町北仰316-1| 祭神=角凝魂神
紀角宿禰神| 社格=式内社
郷社| 創建=創祀年代不詳| 例祭=[[4月18日]]| 神事=}} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町北仰（きとげ）にある神社である。例祭はサンヤレ祭。旧社格は郷社。 ==ご祭神== *主祭神 ** [[角凝魂神]] ** [[紀角宿禰神]] *配祀神 **武内大臣 ==ご神紋== 左三ッ巴 ==歴史== 創祀年代は不詳であるが、角臣来子宿禰によって勧請されたと伝わる。津野郷および河上郷の惣社。[[明治]]14年に郷社に列格された。[[式内社]]論社である。 ==祭事== * サンヤレ祭 ==境内社== * 田部神社 * 稲荷社 * 厳島社 * 北野社 ==ご神徳== * 五穀豊穣 * 家内安全 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:高島市]] [[Category:式内小社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 津野神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:T-tsuno-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]津野神社　遠景 |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町北仰316-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 角凝魂神
紀角宿禰神 |社格 = 式内社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月18日]] |神事 = }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町北仰（きとげ）にある[[神社]]である。[[例祭]]はサンヤレ祭。[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *主祭神 ** [[角凝魂神]] ** [[紀角宿禰|紀角宿禰神]] *配祀神 **[[武内宿禰|武内大臣]] 五穀豊穣、家内安全の神徳を有す。 ==神紋== *左三ッ巴 ==歴史== 創祀年代は不詳であるが、紀角宿彌6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって[[勧請]]されたと伝わる。津野郷および河上郷の[[惣社]]。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に郷社に列格された。[[式内社]][[論社]]である。 == 祭祀 == === 祭事 === * サンヤレ祭 === 社家 === 神職は、荻野家が代々世襲する。 ==境内社== * 田部神社 * 稲荷社 * 厳島社 * 北野社 == 関連項目 == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 今津町角川の津野神社について加筆
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 津野神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:T-tsuno-jinja.JPG|center|250px]]津野神社（今津町北仰） 遠景 |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町北仰316-1（今津町北仰）
滋賀県高島市今津町角川95（今津町角川） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|scale:25000}}（今津町北仰）
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|scale:25000}}（今津町角川） |祭神 = 角凝魂神・紀角宿禰神（今津町北仰）
大国主命・武内宿禰（今津町角川） |社格 = 式内小社・郷社（今津町北仰）
式内小社・村社（今津町角川） |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]]（今津町北仰）
一間社流造（今津町角川） |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月18日]]（今津町北仰）
[[4月27日]]（今津町角川） |神事 = サンヤレ祭（今津町北仰） }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも[[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[近江国]][[高島郡 (滋賀県)|高島郡]]津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === [[紀角|紀角宿禰神]]の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって[[分霊|勧請]]されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の[[総社|惣社]]。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、[[宝永]]元年（[[1704年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に郷社となる。[[角凝魂神]]および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、[[武内宿禰|武内大臣]]（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（[[1876年]]）に村社となる。[[大国主|大国主命]]および[[武内宿禰]]を祭神とする。 == 境内社 == * 今津町北仰:田部神社、稲荷社、厳島社、北野社 ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 == 関連項目 == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221214-01.html 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221214-02.html 津野神社（今津町角川）]（延喜式神社の調査） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:村社]]

[[4月27日]]（今津町角川） |神事 = サンヤレ祭（今津町北仰） }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも[[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[近江国]][[高島郡 (滋賀県)|高島郡]]津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === [[紀角|紀角宿禰神]]の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって[[分霊|勧請]]されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の[[総社|惣社]]。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、[[宝永]]元年（[[1704年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に郷社となる。[[角凝魂神]]および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、[[武内宿禰|武内大臣]]（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（[[1876年]]）に村社となる。[[大国主|大国主命]]および[[武内宿禰]]を祭神とする。 == 境内社 == * 今津町北仰:田部神社、稲荷社、厳島社、北野社 ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 == 関連項目 == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221214-01.html 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221214-02.html 津野神社（今津町角川）]（延喜式神社の調査） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Expand Japanese|津野神社 (高島市)}}

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{Expand Japanese|津野神社 (高島市)}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Tsunou Shrine
| native_name = 津野神社 (高島市)
| image = T-tsuno-jinja.JPG
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tsunou Shrine'''|津野神社 (高島市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]].

（角川）|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=津野神社
（北仰）|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=津野神社
（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも[[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[近江国]][[高島郡 (滋賀県)|高島郡]]津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === [[紀角|紀角宿禰神]]の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって[[分霊|勧請]]されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の[[総社|惣社]]。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、[[宝永]]元年（[[1704年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に郷社となる。[[角凝魂神]]および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、[[武内宿禰|武内大臣]]（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（[[1876年]]）に村社となる。[[大国主|大国主命]]および[[武内宿禰]]を祭神とする。 == 境内社 == * 今津町北仰:田部神社（[[大国主|大国主大神]]）、稲荷社（[[倉稲魂命]]、[[大田命]]、[[大宮売神|大宮姫命]]）、厳島社（[[市杵島姫命]]、[[田心姫|田心比売命]]）、北野社（[[菅原道真|菅原道真公]]） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|scale:25000}}（今津町角川） |祭神 = 角凝魂神・紀角宿禰神（今津町北仰）

（北仰）|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=津野神社
（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも[[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[近江国]][[高島郡 (滋賀県)|高島郡]]津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === [[紀角|紀角宿禰神]]の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって[[分霊|勧請]]されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の[[総社|惣社]]。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、[[宝永]]元年（[[1704年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に郷社となる。[[角凝魂神]]および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、[[武内宿禰|武内大臣]]（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（[[1876年]]）に村社となる。[[大国主|大国主命]]および[[武内宿禰]]を祭神とする。 == 境内社 == * 今津町北仰:田部神社（[[大国主|大国主大神]]）、稲荷社（[[倉稲魂命]]、[[大田命]]、[[大宮売神|大宮姫命]]）、厳島社（[[市杵島姫命]]、[[田心姫|田心比売命]]）、北野社（[[菅原道真|菅原道真公]]） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも[[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[近江国]][[高島郡 (滋賀県)|高島郡]]津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === [[紀角|紀角宿禰神]]の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって[[分霊|勧請]]されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の[[総社|惣社]]。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、[[宝永]]元年（[[1704年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に郷社となる。[[角凝魂神]]および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、[[武内宿禰|武内大臣]]（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（[[1876年]]）に村社となる。[[大国主|大国主命]]および[[武内宿禰]]を祭神とする。 == 境内社 == * 今津町北仰:田部神社（[[大国主|大国主大神]]）、稲荷社（[[倉稲魂命]]、[[大田命]]、[[大宮売神|大宮姫命]]）、厳島社（[[市杵島姫命]]、[[田心姫|田心比売命]]）、北野社（[[菅原道真|菅原道真公]]） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

[[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[JA:津野神社 (高島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11556457}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|津野神社 (高島市)|version=107290966|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 津野神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:T-tsuno-jinja.JPG|270px]]
津野神社（今津町北仰）遠景 |所在地 = {{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}今津町北仰316-1（今津町北仰）
滋賀県高島市今津町角川95（今津町角川） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|scale:25000}}（今津町北仰）
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|scale:25000}}（今津町角川） |祭神 = 角凝魂神・紀角宿禰神（今津町北仰）
大国主命・武内宿禰（今津町角川） |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社}}（今津町北仰）
{{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|村社}}（今津町角川） |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684|三間社流造}}（今津町北仰）
一間社流造（今津町角川） |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月18日|Q2521}}（今津町北仰）
{{jalink|4月27日|Q2533}}（今津町角川） |神事 = サンヤレ祭（今津町北仰） |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Shiga|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|}}|position=top|label=津野神社
（北仰）|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=津野神社
（角川）|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=津野神社
（北仰）|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=津野神社
（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}にある{{jalink|近江国|Q843882}}{{jalink|高島郡 (滋賀県)|Q11669692|高島郡}}津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === {{jalink|紀角|Q11605107|紀角宿禰神}}の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって{{jalink|分霊|Q195793|勧請}}されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129|惣社}}。{{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、{{jalink|宝永|Q1145262}}元年（{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に郷社となる。{{jalink|角凝魂神}}および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668|武内大臣}}（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（{{jalink|1876年|Q7766}}）に村社となる。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}および{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}を祭神とする。 == 境内社 == * 今津町北仰:田部神社（{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主大神}}）、稲荷社（{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}、{{jalink|大田命}}、{{jalink|大宮売神||大宮姫命}}）、厳島社（{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}、{{jalink|田心姫||田心比売命}}）、北野社（{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真公}}） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

滋賀県高島市今津町角川95（今津町角川） |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|scale:25000}}（今津町北仰）
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|scale:25000}}（今津町角川） |祭神 = 角凝魂神・紀角宿禰神（今津町北仰）
大国主命・武内宿禰（今津町角川） |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}（今津町北仰）
{{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（今津町角川） |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684|三間社流造}}（今津町北仰）

{{jalink|4月27日|Q2533}}（今津町角川） |神事 = サンヤレ祭（今津町北仰） |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Shiga|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|}}|position=top|label=津野神社
（北仰）|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=津野神社
（角川）|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=津野神社
（北仰）|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=津野神社
（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}にある{{jalink|近江国|Q843882}}{{jalink|高島郡 (滋賀県)|Q11669692|高島郡}}津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === {{jalink|紀角|Q11605107|紀角宿禰神}}の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって{{jalink|分霊|Q195793|勧請}}されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129|惣社}}。{{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、{{jalink|宝永|Q1145262}}元年（{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に郷社となる。{{jalink|角凝魂神}}および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668|武内大臣}}（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（{{jalink|1876年|Q7766}}）に村社となる。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}および{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}を祭神とする。 === 境内社 === * 今津町北仰:田部神社（{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主大神}}）、稲荷社（{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}、{{jalink|大田命}}、{{jalink|大宮売神||大宮姫命}}）、厳島社（{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}、{{jalink|田心姫||田心比売命}}）、北野社（{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真公}}） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}にある{{jalink|近江国|Q843882}}{{jalink|高島郡 (滋賀県)|Q11669692|高島郡}}津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === {{jalink|紀角|Q11605107|紀角宿禰神}}の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって{{jalink|分霊|Q195793|勧請}}されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129|惣社}}。{{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、{{jalink|宝永|Q1145262}}元年（{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に郷社となる。{{jalink|角凝魂神}}および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668|武内大臣}}（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（{{jalink|1876年|Q7766}}）に村社となる。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}および{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}を祭神とする。 === 境内社 === * 今津町北仰:田部神社（{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主大神}}）、稲荷社（{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}、{{jalink|大田命}}、{{jalink|大宮売神||大宮姫命}}）、厳島社（{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}、{{jalink|田心姫||田心比売命}}）、北野社（{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真公}}） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}にある{{jalink|近江国|Q843882}}{{jalink|高島郡 (滋賀県)|Q11669692|高島郡}}津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === {{jalink|紀角|Q11605107|紀角宿禰神}}の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって{{jalink|分霊|Q195793|勧請}}されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129|惣社}}。{{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、{{jalink|宝永|Q1145262}}元年（{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に郷社となる。{{jalink|角凝魂神}}および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668|武内大臣}}（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（{{jalink|1876年|Q7766}}）に村社となる。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}および{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}を祭神とする。 === 境内社 === * 今津町北仰:田部神社（{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主大神}}）、稲荷社（{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}、{{jalink|大田命}}、{{jalink|大宮売神||大宮姫命}}）、厳島社（{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}、{{jalink|田心姫||田心比売命}}）、北野社（{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真公}}） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

大国主命・武内宿禰（今津町角川） |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}（今津町北仰）
{{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}・旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（今津町角川） |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684|三間社流造}}（今津町北仰）
一間社流造（今津町角川） |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月18日|Q2521}}（今津町北仰）
{{jalink|4月27日|Q2533}}（今津町角川） |神事 = サンヤレ祭（今津町北仰） |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Shiga|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|}}|position=top|label=津野神社
（北仰）|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=津野神社
（角川）|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=津野神社
（北仰）|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=津野神社
（角川）|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''津野神社'''（つのうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === 創建の年代は不詳である。高島市の津野神社は今津町に2か所あり、今津町北仰と今津町角川にある津野神社はいずれも{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}にある{{jalink|近江国|Q843882}}{{jalink|高島郡 (滋賀県)|Q11669692|高島郡}}津野神社の後裔社とされる。 === 今津町北仰 === {{jalink|紀角|Q11605107|紀角宿禰神}}の6世の孫、角臣来子宿彌によって{{jalink|分霊|Q195793|勧請}}されたと伝えられる。津野郷および河上郷の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129|惣社}}。{{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}には兵火により社殿が焼失したが、{{jalink|宝永|Q1145262}}元年（{{jalink|1704年|Q6824}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に郷社となる。{{jalink|角凝魂神}}および紀角宿禰神を主祭神とし、{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668|武内大臣}}（武内宿禰）を配祀する。 === 今津町角川 === 今津町北仰の津野神社と同様に戦国時代には兵火により社殿が焼失している。明治9年（{{jalink|1876年|Q7766}}）に村社となる。{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}}および{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668}}を祭神とする。 === 境内社 === * 今津町北仰:田部神社（{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主大神}}）、稲荷社（{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}、{{jalink|大田命}}、{{jalink|大宮売神||大宮姫命}}）、厳島社（{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}、{{jalink|田心姫||田心比売命}}）、北野社（{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真公}}） ** 田部神社は延喜式神名帳にある近江国高島郡鎮座の田部神社の論社 * 今津町角川:山王大宮 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）]（滋賀県神社庁） *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）]（滋賀県神社庁） {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|scale:25000}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |祭神 = Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami / Kinotsuno Sukune-no-Kami (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)

{{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287}} / former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684|three-bay nagare-zukuri}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
one-bay nagare-zukuri (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|April 18|Q2521}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
{{jalink|April 27|Q2533}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |神事 = Sanyare Festival (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō) |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Shiga|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|}}|position=top|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Takashima|Q845794}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{jalink|Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|Takashima District}}, {{jalink|Ōmi Province|Q843882}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{jalink|Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129|sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{jalink|Hōei|Q1145262}} 1 ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}). It became a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}). It enshrines {{jalink|Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{jalink|Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}). It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōta-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto||Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto||Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Takashima|Q845794}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{jalink|Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|Takashima District}}, {{jalink|Ōmi Province|Q843882}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{jalink|Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129|sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{jalink|Hōei|Q1145262}} 1 ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}). It became a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}). It enshrines {{jalink|Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{jalink|Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}). It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōta-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto||Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto||Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

(Kita-gyō)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Takashima|Q845794}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{jalink|Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|Takashima District}}, {{jalink|Ōmi Province|Q843882}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{jalink|Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129|sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{jalink|Hōei|Q1145262}} 1 ({{jalink|1704|Q6824}}). It became a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}). It enshrines {{jalink|Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{jalink|Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{jalink|1876|Q7766}}). It enshrines {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{jalink|Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōta-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto||Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto||Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}). It enshrines {{ill||lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{ill||lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill||lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(Kita-gyō)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=April 27|Q2533|WD=Q2533}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |神事 = Sanyare Festival (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō) |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Shiga|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|}}|position=top|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(Kita-gyō)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha|1=Sōja shrine}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsunou Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:T-tsuno-jinja.JPG|270px]]
Tsunou Shrine (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō) distant view |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}} Imazu-chō Kita-gyō 316-1 (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|scale:25000}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |祭神 = Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami / Kinotsuno Sukune-no-Kami (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto / Takeuchi Sukune (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
{{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
one-bay nagare-zukuri (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
{{ill|lt=April 27|Q2533|WD=Q2533|1=April 27}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |神事 = Sanyare Festival (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō) |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Shiga|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|}}|position=top|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami|1=Ki no Tsuno}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha|1=Sōja shrine}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami|1=Ki no Tsuno}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha|1=Sōja shrine}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsunou Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:T-tsuno-jinja.JPG|270px]]
Tsunou Shrine (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō) distant view |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}} Imazu-chō Kita-gyō 316-1 (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
Shiga Prefecture Takashima City Imazu-chō Tsunokawa 95 (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|scale:25000}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|scale:25000}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |祭神 = Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami / Kinotsuno Sukune-no-Kami (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto / Takeuchi Sukune (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
{{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
one-bay nagare-zukuri (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō)
{{ill|lt=April 27|Q2533|WD=Q2533|1=April 27}} (Imazu-chō Tsunokawa) |神事 = Sanyare Festival (Imazu-chō Kita-gyō) |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Shiga|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E|}}|position=top|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Shiga|coordinates={{coord2|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E|}}|position=bottom|label=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami|1=Ki no Tsuno}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha|1=Sōja shrine}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

(Tsunokawa)|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35|25|18|N|135|59|0|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|25|21.2|N|136|01|53.4|E}}|label1=Tsunou Shrine
(Kita-gyō)|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|25|15|N|135|56|59|E}}|label2=Tsunou Shrine
(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami|1=Ki no Tsuno}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha|1=Sōja shrine}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}). It became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

(Tsunokawa)|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Tsunou Shrine''' (つのうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. There are two Tsunou Shrines in Imazu-chō, Takashima City; both the shrine in Imazu-chō Kita-gyō and the one in Imazu-chō Tsunokawa are regarded as descendant shrines of Tsunou Shrine in {{ill|lt=Takashima District (Shiga)|Q11669692|WD=Q11669692|lt=Takashima District|1=Takashima District, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Imazu-chō Kita-gyō === It is said to have been invited by Kadono Omi no Kishi no Sukune, sixth-generation grandson of {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsuno|Q11605107|WD=Q11605107|lt=Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami|1=Ki no Tsuno}}. It was the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|lt=sōsha|1=Sōja shrine}} of Tsunou-go and Kawakami-go. In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine buildings burned down in warfare, but were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|WD=Q6824|1=1704}}). It enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori Mitama-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and Ki no Tsuno Sukune-no-Kami as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Ōomi|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (Takeuchi Sukune) as associated deity. === Imazu-chō Tsunokawa === Like the Kita-gyō Tsunou Shrine, its shrine buildings were burned in warfare in the Sengoku period. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}). It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. === Precinct shrines === * Imazu-chō Kita-gyō: Tabe Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi Ōkami|1=Ōkuninushi}}), Inari-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto}}), Itsukushima-sha ({{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagorihime-no-Mikoto}}), Kitano-sha ({{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}) ** Tabe Shrine is a candidate for Tabe Shrine in Takashima District, Ōmi Province, listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō * Imazu-chō Tsunokawa: Sannō Ōmiya === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1339 津野神社（今津町北仰）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1352 津野神社（今津町角川）] (Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsunou Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]
